props.caption: Twenty-nine countries signed a China-headquartered World AI Cooperation Organization while New York and Australia moved to govern datacenters as territory — AI hardening into bloc geopolitics.

items.note: Spain and Argentina meet at MetLife at 3 p.m. ET under an FBI-led operation its director called the biggest in the bureau’s history, with the result still hours away.
Iran is led by Mojtaba Khamenei, appointed in March after a U.S.–Israeli strike killed his father, Ali Khamenei, on 28 February, the killing that opened this war.
Tanker traffic has fallen to a two-month low and the buffers that absorbed the war’s first supply loss are drained, keeping a risk premium in crude.
Twenty-nine countries signed a founding agreement for a China-headquartered World AI Cooperation Organization, hardening artificial intelligence into bloc geopolitics.
Two U.S. service members were killed and one went missing on 17 July defending a base against Iranian missiles and drones, the strike that set off the latest U.S. wave.

items.what: Russian ballistic missiles struck Kyiv overnight into 19 July, killing at least one person. The attack stood as the sharpest non-Middle-East conflict signal of the day and a fresh reminder of the air-defence bottleneck.

Changxin Memory Technologies, a Chinese DRAM maker, drew heavy institutional demand for its roughly $8.6 billion Shanghai listing due 27 July. Global chip-stock selling nonetheless dented the offering.

Hungary’s president signed a law change ending his own term early, deepening a constitutional rupture that has drawn scrutiny from the Venice Commission. The episode remains a major under-covered European rule-of-law story.

Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-1 lifted off from Sriharikota on 18 July, the first orbital launch by a private Indian company from Indian soil. The flight marked a real threshold for the country’s private aerospace ambitions.

West African heads of state convened the bloc’s 69th summit in Freetown on 19 July. A rotating-chair handover is expected as the group weighs its fractured relationship with the Sahel states that quit last year.
The ferry MV Barima, carrying 116 people, capsized near Iron Punt on the night of 18 July. By 19 July afternoon between 53 and 67 had been rescued and a state-led search continued for the rest.
